title: Imperialism: debates
description: Test your knowledge on the political, cultural, and economic interactions between empires and nations in the early 1900s!

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9e1366c882b622ead94e8d6d732aee4b.jpg)

>*Caption: "Showing how Uncle Sam has been an expansionist first, last, and all the time."*

>-Source: "Lesson for anti-expansionists," *Judge Magazine,* [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lesson_for_anti_expansionists.jpg) 1899

**Which of the following best describes the message in the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: As the United States has continued to expand, it has become more powerful.
The acquisition of more territory has made the United States violate its own morals.
The United States should refuse to make peacetime alliances with foreign powers.
As the United States has industrialized, the country has become more powerful.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9e1366c882b622ead94e8d6d732aee4b.jpg)

>*Caption: "Showing how Uncle Sam has been an expansionist first, last, and all the time."*

>-Source: "Lesson for anti-expansionists," *Judge Magazine,* [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lesson_for_anti_expansionists.jpg) 1899

**The message portrayed in the political cartoon has the most in common with which of the following developments in other periods in United States history?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the acquisition of territory in the early to mid-nineteenth century under the belief that it was America’s God-given right to expand
the attempts by the federal government to limit immigration from Asian countries in the late nineteenth century
the emergence of voluntary organizations focused on improving the lives of recent immigrants in the mid- to late nineteenth century
the extension of civil rights to formerly enslaved persons of African descent following the Civil War

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9e1366c882b622ead94e8d6d732aee4b.jpg)

>*Caption: "Showing how Uncle Sam has been an expansionist first, last, and all the time."*

>-Source: "Lesson for anti-expansionists," *Judge Magazine,* [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lesson_for_anti_expansionists.jpg) 1899

**The image most directly illustrates a United States foreign policy that emphasized which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: expanding American territorial holdings
accepting peacetime alliances
reducing the size of the American military
entering into a collective security arrangement with western Europe

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4942e2cc67810210e7da45ff6c1e874e.jpg)

>-Source: John T. McCutcheon, "What the United States has Fought For," [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Free_from_Spanish.jpg) 1914

**The image most directly reflects which of the following popular beliefs of the early twentieth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States has a duty to educate and spread Christianity to the former Spanish colonies and Hawaii.
The former Spanish colonies and Hawaii should have the right to govern themselves because the US was founded on the idea of popular sovereignty.
The United States should avoid peacetime alliances with other countries to reduce foreign entanglements.
The former Spanish colonies and Hawaii should join the United States as new states so that the American economy can expand to foreign markets.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4942e2cc67810210e7da45ff6c1e874e.jpg)

>-Source: John T. McCutcheon, "What the United States has Fought For," [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Free_from_Spanish.jpg) 1914

**The message portrayed by the artist of this cartoon had most in common with which of the following ideas?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Social Darwinism
Populism
Nativism
Communism

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4942e2cc67810210e7da45ff6c1e874e.jpg)

>-Source: John T. McCutcheon, "What the United States has Fought For," [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Free_from_Spanish.jpg) 1914

**Which of the following arguments about American imperialism does the image most strongly support?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It helped the acquired territories develop into “civilized” countries.
It violated the American value of popular sovereignty.
It weakened the United States’ ability to protect itself from foreign influence.
It led to a stronger economy because it opened access to foreign markets.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2625460e5b9d50dd7920c49c0705a3fb.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Roosevelt_monroe_Doctrine_cartoon.jpg) 1906

**The image most directly illustrates a United States foreign policy that emphasized which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: opposition to European intervention in the Western hemisphere
support for a war with Britain to secure additional territory in the Caribbean
opposition to the United States making permanent alliances with other countries
support for the colonization of Latin American countries to spread Christianity

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2625460e5b9d50dd7920c49c0705a3fb.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Roosevelt_monroe_Doctrine_cartoon.jpg) 1906

**The situation shown in the image depicts which of the following trends in the early twentieth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the United States’ efforts to act as an international police force in the Western Hemisphere to defend Latin America from European powers
the development of an isolationist foreign policy that was meant to protect the United States from involvement in world affairs
the growth in the president’s power to influence foreign policy and deploy troops without getting congressional approval
the emergence of a group of reformers who sought to educate and convert people in Central America to Christianity

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2625460e5b9d50dd7920c49c0705a3fb.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Roosevelt_monroe_Doctrine_cartoon.jpg) 1906

**The image most directly reflects which of the following popular beliefs of the early twentieth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States was responsible for protecting Latin America from European interference and colonization.
The United States needed to increase the size of its Navy to become a legitimate world power.
The United States had a duty to spread Christianity to countries in Asia and Latin America.
The United States should refuse to colonize other countries because colonization went against popular sovereignty.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a820c756ab0da130a67826c75cdb591f.png)

Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Philippine-American_War.png)

**The message portrayed by the artist of this cartoon had most in common with the ideas of which of the following groups?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Anti-imperialists
Imperialists
Populists
Capitalists

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a820c756ab0da130a67826c75cdb591f.png)

Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Philippine-American_War.png)

**Which of the following best describes the message in the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States’ attempts at imperialism is going to harm the United States because the Philippines does not want to be controlled.
The acquisition of foreign territories, like the Philippines, will only make the United States stronger over time.
The Philippines is willingly joining the United States to gain the benefits of statehood.
The acquisition of the Philippines will make it easier to trade with large Asian countries, like China.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a820c756ab0da130a67826c75cdb591f.png)

Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Philippine-American_War.png)

**The perspective in the image best illustrates which of the following changes in United States’ foreign policy in the early twentieth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a transition from an isolationist foreign policy to an interventionist foreign policy
a transition from an interventionist  foreign policy to an isolationist foreign policy
a transition from an isolationist foreign policy to a laissez-faire foreign policy
a transition from a laissez-faire foreign policy to an interventionist foreign policy
